title: orthaea minor
intro: Orthaea minor, also known as the "Queen of the Andes," is a species of orchid native to the Andean region of South America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and provide relief from coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, Orthaea minor is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
